speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. I see you're currently on Lisinopril 20mg once daily, started back on August 25th.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that's right — I've been taking it every morning with breakfast.

speaker: doctor
text: How have you been feeling since starting it? Any new symptoms or concerns?

speaker: patient
text: Actually, I’ve noticed some blurred vision over the past few days — it’s been a bit bothersome, especially when reading or driving at night.

speaker: doctor
text: Let me check your vitals. Your diastolic blood pressure today is 75 mmHg — that’s within normal range.

speaker: patient
text: That’s reassuring. But I’ve also felt unusually tired and a little achy lately.

speaker: doctor
text: Any fever, chills, or cough?

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is elevated at 38.5 °C — looks like you have a low-grade fever.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve had a sore throat since yesterday and my neck feels stiff.

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure is 156 mmHg — slightly elevated for today.

speaker: doctor
text: Hmm, let me recheck — actually, this time it’s 133 mmHg. That’s a notable drop from the first reading just now.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been dizzy when standing up quickly — maybe that’s related?

speaker: doctor
text: Given your current lipid profile and recent BP variability, I’m going to switch you from Lisinopril to Atorvastatin 40mg taken at night instead — we’ll hold off on the ACE inhibitor for now while we investigate further.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic pressure is now down to 61 mmHg — that’s lower than before and may explain your dizziness and visual changes.

speaker: patient
text: That makes sense — the blurriness does seem worse when I stand up.

speaker: doctor
text: We should also rule out an infection contributing to your fever and fatigue. I’ll order a CBC and strep test.

speaker: patient
text: Okay, thank you. Should I stop the Lisinopril right away?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes — discontinue it today. And your temperature has come down slightly to 37.6 °C, which is encouraging but still mildly elevated.

speaker: patient
text: I checked my BP at home this morning — same as the first reading you got: 156 over something.

speaker: patient
text: And my thermometer still shows 38.5 °C — that’s the same number you saw earlier, not the lower one just now.
